cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out between what times Richard could have gone to the basketball court today. The options are given as (A) 3pm to 7pm, (B) 5am to 7am, (C) 7pm to 8pm, and (D) 7am to 3pm. Elizabeth saw Richard taking photos near the Leaning Tower of Pisa from 5am to 7am. 3. Lisa saw Richard fixing their computer at the electronic store from 3pm to 7pm. 4. Leslie saw Richard reading at the library from 7pm to 8pm. 5. The basketball court was closed after 8pm. So the question is: Between what times could Richard have gone to the basketball court? The key here is to determine the time periods when Richard was not doing any of the activities mentioned above and could have gone to the basketball court before it closed at 8pm. Let's break it down step by step. First, Richard woke up at 5am. That just tells us when he started being active for the day, but doesn't directly restrict when he could go to the basketball court unless we assume he couldn't go before he woke up. However, the problem doesn't say the basketball court is closed before a certain time, so unless there's a restriction, we can't assume he couldn't go before 5am. Now, let's look at the time intervals during which Richard was seen in other places: - From 5am to 7am: Taking photos near the Leaning Tower of Pisa (Elizabeth). - From 3pm to 7pm: Fixing computer at the electronic store (Lisa). - From 7pm to 8pm: Reading at the library (Leslie). The basketball court was closed after 8pm, so Richard had to go before 8pm. So let's visualize the possible time periods: Time of day: 5am (wake up) - 7am (photos) - 7am - 3pm (?) - 3pm (computer) - 7pm (computer ends) - 7pm - 8pm (library) - 8pm (court closed). So from 5am to 7am, he was taking photos. From 3pm to 7pm, he was fixing the computer. From 7pm to 8pm, he was at the library. The basketball court was closed after 8pm, so he had to go before 8pm.
